body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Reasoning

Codex (0.130.0) ships **no built-in autonomous self-prompting loop** — there is no `/goal`-style construct like Claude Code's Stop-hook re-prompt, no Plandex-style full-auto, and `autonomous_mode` is not among codex's declared capabilities (capabilities.json has no autonomous_mode key; the closest, headless_mode, is single-shot `codex exec`). Codex cannot drive ITSELF through an entire task goal with no user prompts between iterations.

What codex CAN model — and what the scenario-meanings 'Essence' actually targets for an adapter that lacks native autonomy — is an **externally-driven loop**: a driver/harness feeds successive prompts to the same live REPL session in a loop, each prompt advancing one iteration of a single ongoing goal. This is the honest codex analogue of the autonomous-loop primitive: the irrlicht-relevant behavior is whether the session cycles cleanly across internally-similar, back-to-back iterations without getting stuck in working and without false flips, NOT whether the model self-prompts.

So agent_supports is **partial**: codex supports the loop only when the loop body is supplied externally (driver send → wait_turn → short sleep → send …), not autonomously. This mirrors how the claudecode sibling already had to relax the spec's strict 'stays continuously working' bullet: even claudecode's /goal flickers ready↔working per Stop-hook iteration. Codex's externally-driven loop produces the same observable arc — a chain of clean per-iteration working→ready cycles on one rollout session — so it is captured the same way the claudecode sibling captures it.

## Observability

irrlicht_observes: **yes**. Each loop iteration is a normal codex turn on the live rollout session: ready → working at iteration start, working → ready at iteration end. The daemon sees the proc-PID pre-session born ready, the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, pid bound, then N clean per-iteration working/ready arcs on the SAME rollout session_id, then process_exited when the driver tears the REPL down. No `waiting` state (codex has no permission gates: permission_hooks=false). This is structurally identical to the already-recorded codex multi-turn-conversation cell, which validates the same per-turn arc shape — the only semantic difference is intent (loop iterations of one goal vs three unrelated prompts), which the spec captures as a no-false-flip / no-stuck-working invariant. The thing irrlicht does NOT surface — a first-class 'iteration 3/N' counter — is not a codex construct at all (no goal_status attachment) and is out of scope for this primitive.

## Why not n/a

Not n/a: the externally-driven multi-turn loop is fully expressible with codex's interactive driver (send/slash/wait_turn/sleep all supported) and the resulting recording exercises exactly the irrlicht primitive the scenario cares about (clean cross-iteration cycling, no stuck-working, no false waiting). It is NOT an orchestrator-only concept (gastown) — a single codex session iterating under external prompting is a legitimate single-agent recording.
caveats: Codex has no native autonomous mode; the loop body must be supplied externally by the driver (send → wait_turn → sleep, repeated). The recording models an externally-driven loop, not codex self-prompting. Documented in the recipe description so a re-record diff doesn't read as a regression.

Spec strict reading ('state stays working for the entire run') does not hold for codex any more than it does for claudecode: the session cycles ready↔working per externally-fed iteration. The recording asserts clean per-iteration cycling plus the no-waiting / no-stuck invariants, not a single continuous working span.
Mind the classifier debounce: ≥4s sleep between wait_turn and the next send so consecutive iterations register as distinct working→ready arcs rather than one coalesced turn.

sources.note: No autonomous_mode key; headless_mode is single-shot codex exec, permission_hooks=false (no waiting gates), slash_commands=true, session_resume=true.

Essence + conceptual flow: the key irrlicht primitive is keeping/cycling session state across internally-generated iteration boundaries with no false flips. Strict 'stays working' bullet relaxed for adapters whose iterations are turn-delimited.

Sibling models the loop as per-iteration working→ready arcs (not a continuous working span) because Stop hooks re-prompt after end_turn — same observable shape the externally-driven codex loop produces.
Driver supports send|slash, wait_turn, sleep, interrupt, keys, reset_session, fork, exit_clean, resume — every step type the externally-driven loop recipe needs.
